Four structural groups: Group 1 (2 stations) direct street<->platform per direction, no mezzanine; Group 2 (4 stations) street/mezzanine combined + per-direction platform leg; Group 3 (8 stations) shared street<->mezzanine prerequisite + 2 per-direction mezzanine<->platform legs; Group 4 (7 stations) straight 2-elevator chain, single path (non-standard-levels was just unusual level naming). resolution.notes: Batch 3 (21 WMATA stations), approved by Bryce as a group 2026-07-15. Four structural groups: Group 1 (2 stations) direct street<->platform per direction, no mezzanine; Group 2 (4 stations) street/mezzanine combined + per-direction platform leg; Group 3 (8 stations) shared street<->mezzanine prerequisite + 2 per-direction mezzanine<->platform legs; Group 4 (7 stations) straight 2-elevator chain, single path (non-standard-levels was just unusual level naming). No redundancy claimed anywhere. Several elevators upgraded from synthetic GTFS slot ids to real observed UnitNames (checked against wmata-data/observed-units.json) during this pass. Confidence 8/10 (Groups 2-4) / 7/10 (Group 1). Implemented in src/catalog/wmata-models.ts.
